summary: Security hardening verified: server-side paid chapter content gating works end-to-end. Wrote 14 new pytest cases in test_chapter_gating_security.py covering /api/books stripping (both seeded books), /api/books/{slug} stripping, admin /api/admin/books untouched (full content), new /api/reader/chapter/{slug}/{cid} for guest/user-0bal/user-+bal/blocked/admin/404 paths, plus a RAW body-snippet guard that greps the full JSON response text so no paid body can hide in any field. All 14 gating tests pass + 43 previous Phase 1/2 regression tests pass (57/57 total). Frontend verified with Playwright: guest on paid chapter shows reader-locked-overlay with reader-locked-auth_required + overlay-signin and the distinctive paid snippet 'Pricing is the first place a founder learns' is NOT present anywhere in document.body; 0-balance user shows reader-locked-insufficient_reading_time + overlay-buy-time, no body leak; positive-balance user reads full content with reader-time-pill visible; admin token unlocks paid chapter in /reader; legacy /signin redirects to /login; /pricing renders pack-30m/1h/3h/10h.

verified_test_cases.backend_phase1_phase2_regression: 43/43 PASS (all prior tests from backend_test.py and test_phase2_user_reader.py still green)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py now ~1295 lines — still monolithic. _strip_paid_chapter_content() + new /reader/chapter/{slug}/{cid} gating logic is clean and colocated with session/heartbeat code; splitting into /routes/reader.py would improve maintainability but not urgent.
_strip_paid_chapter_content() correctly shallow-copies chapter dicts before blanking content so the in-memory DB cache is not mutated — good.
Gating endpoint uses optional_principal which accepts both admin and user JWTs and degrades to None for guests. Admin short-circuit happens BEFORE any balance check — correct.
Blocked-user check happens BEFORE balance check — blocked takes precedence even if balance>0. Verified.
Guard test (raw response.text) adds a defense-in-depth check so future refactors (e.g., adding a 'preview' field that echoes content) cannot accidentally leak paid body anywhere in the JSON.
UI: Reader.jsx uses admin-token-first fallback (reads earnalism_admin_token from localStorage if present, otherwise earnalism_user_token). Reasonable for admin-preview-from-admin-console workflow.
Nit: the locked overlay renders `reader-locked-${reason.toLowerCase()}` — lowercased AUTH_REQUIRED becomes 'auth_required'. Matches what the request specified. Good.

Minor UX: when a logged-in reader hits a paid chapter at 0 balance, the 402 from /reader/session/start is logged as a console error. Not user-visible and does not break the overlay flow, but could be downgraded to console.info to reduce noise.
test_credentials: admin@theearnalism.com / Earnalism@2026 verified. reader@earnalism.com / Reader@2026 verified. Fresh signup fixture creates gate-<ts>-<rand>@test.com / TestPass123.
seed_data_creation: Tests created a throwaway user via /users/signup, toggled its balance (+60min then drained) and briefly blocked/unblocked it. Reader (reader@earnalism.com) balance ended at 0 (same as seed default). A handful of TEST_ wallet_transactions rows exist on the throwaway user — harmless since the user is never reused.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Chapter-gating security is locked in. To retest: set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` — all 57 should pass. The single most important guard is test_public_book_body_does_not_contain_paid_content_raw which greps response.text for a distinctive paid-chapter snippet — if anyone ever re-exposes chapter bodies via the public catalog API, this will fail loudly. Admin view (/api/admin/books) deliberately keeps full content — do NOT add stripping there. Reader UI relies on chapterPayload.chapter.content ONLY (no fallback to book.chapters[*].content), so the UI cannot accidentally render leaked content even if the public API regressed. Paid snippet used for DOM checks: 'Pricing is the first place a founder learns'.
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration validated a security hardening that was already implemented; no regressions found.
